reasoning: The ICE BofA US Corporate Index OAS is currently at ~1.06% (as of early May 2025), which represents an extremely tight level historically. Spreads face an asymmetric boundary: they cannot tighten substantially below ~0.80%-0.90%, but can widen rapidly during minor risk-off events or macroeconomic shifts. Although short-term momentum may be sideways or slightly tightening, any random shock is more likely to cause a disproportionate upward move. Over short horizons, financial time series approximate a random walk, with a slight upward bias here due to the asymmetric downside bound. In accordance with the system instruction that uncertainty must increase and probabilities for distant dates MUST be closer to 0.5, I assign a modest premium for widening in the near-term and steadily pull the probability down precisely toward 0.50 across the progressive forecast horizons (1 week, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months).
